data.title: D.R. Horton announces pricing of $700 million of 5.500% senior notes due 2035
data.description:
• D.R. Horton (NYSE:DHI) on Wednesday said that it has priced a registered underwritten public offering of $700 million aggregate principal amount of 5.500% senior notes due 2035.
  • The senior notes will pay interest semi-annually at a rate of 5.500% per year and will mature on October 15, 2035.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on February 26, 2025.
  • D.R. Horton (NYSE:DHI) intends to use the net proceeds of the offering for general corporate purposes.

data.title: KKR buys 58% of Japan's FujiSoft for $4.4B, outbidding Bain Capital
SanDisk set to join S&P SmallCap 600
data.description:
• SanDisk (SNDK) will replace Leslie (LESL) in the S&P SmallCap 600 effective before trading on Tuesday, February 25.
  • S&P 500 constituent Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) is spinning off SanDisk (SNDK) in a transaction expected to be completed on February 24.
  • Western Digital will remain in the S&P 500 post-spin-off.
  • Leslie's market capitalization no longer represents the small cap market space.

data.title: SBM Offshore reports FY results
data.description:
• SBM Offshore press release (OTCPK:SBFFF): FY GAAP EPS of $0.84.
  • Revenue of $4.78B (-3.6% Y/Y).
  • Record Directional Revenue of US$6.1 billion (+35%), in line with guidance
  • Record Directional EBITDA of US$1.9 billion (+44%), in line with guidance
  • Record US$35.1 billion Directional backlog; US$9.5 billion or EUR51.6/share2 Directional net cash backlog3
  • 30% increase in cash return to US$1.59 per share4: US$155 million dividend; US$150 million share repurchase

data.title: Teck Resources reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Teck Resources press release (NYSE:TECK): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.45.
  • Revenue of C$2.79B (+51.6% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA of C$835 million

data.title: AEGON reports 2H results
data.description:
• AEGON press release (NYSE:AEG): 2H Net profit of EUR 741 million as operating result and benefit from the a.s.r. stake are partly offset by restructuring charges and net impairments in the US
  • Operating result of EUR 776 million, up 14% compared with the second half of 2023, reflecting improved experience variance in the US and business growth in the US and asset management
  • Shareholders’ equity per share of EUR 4.53, increases by 13% compared with June 30, 2024, while contractual service margin per share after estimated tax adjustment increases by 5% to EUR 4.38. Valuation equity per share – the sum of these components – grew by 9% to EUR 8.91

data.title: Gerdau reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Gerdau press release (NYSE:GGB): Q4 Adjusted EBITDA of R$ 2.4B, up 17.2% from 4Q23.
  • Investments (CAPEX) amounted to R$2.4 billion in 4Q24, totaling R$6.2 billion for 2024.
  • For 2025, the comapny estimates an investment of R$6 billion in projects allocated to maintenance and competitiveness.

data.title: Calibre Mining reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Calibre Mining press release (OTCQX:CXBMF): Q4 Revenue of $202.97M (+33.9% Y/Y) misses by $0.63M.
  • Record consolidated Q4 gold production of 76,269 ounces, 2025 off to a strong start.
  • Consolidated FY 2024 gold production of 242,487 ounces, exceeding updated 2024 guidance.

data.title: Taseko Mines reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Taseko Mines press release (NYSE:TGB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.03.
  • Revenue of C$167.8M (+9.2% Y/Y).
  • Total operating costs for the quarter were US$2.42 per pound produced
  • The Company had a cash balance of $173 million and approximately $331 million of available liquidity at December 31, 2024.

data.title: Centerspace outlines 2025 core FFO midpoint of $4.98 amid growth initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Centerspace (NYSE:CSR) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Anne Olson, CEO, highlighted that 2024 was marked by stable revenue growth, strong occupancy, and diligent expense management. She noted that the company achieved $4.88 of core FFO per share, driven by sector-leading same-store NOI growth and increased occupancy to 95.5%, a 70 basis point improvement over the previous year.
  • Centerspace expanded its portfolio by acquiring The Lydian in Denver and redeeming Series B preferred shares to improve the leverage profile. A quarterly dividend increase to $0.77 per share was also announced.
  • Olson emphasized the strong fundamentals of the Midwest market, noting lower supply and stable growth. North Dakota led with blended spreads of 4.4%, followed by Nebraska and Rochester at 2.5% and 2.3%, respectively.
  • CFO Bhairav Patel reported Q4 core FFO of $1.21 per share, attributed to a 3.1% increase in same-store revenue and a 70 basis point year-over-year occupancy increase. Same-store expenses rose by 4.6%, primarily due to higher controllable costs, including repairs and maintenance.
  • Grant Campbell, SVP of Investments, discussed the resilience of apartment demand and a tapering supply profile, particularly in Minneapolis and Denver. He noted that transaction volumes remain muted due to interest rate volatility.

Outlook

  • Centerspace projects 2025 core FFO at $4.98 per share at the midpoint, reflecting a 2% annual growth. Same-store NOI is expected to rise by 2.25%, with same-store revenue growth of 2.5% and expense growth of 3%.
  • Blended leasing spreads are anticipated at 2.4%, with renewals expected at approximately 3% and new leases in the high 1% to 2% range. Occupancy is forecasted to remain stable at 2024 levels.
  • The company plans $16 million to $18 million in value-add expenditures and recurring CapEx per home averaging $1,150 per unit. Benefits from centralization and a favorable insurance renewal are expected to curtail expense growth.

Financial Results

  • Centerspace reported Q4 core FFO of $1.21 per share, driven by a 2.1% increase in same-store NOI and a 3.1% rise in same-store revenue compared to Q4 2023. Occupancy reached 95.5%.
  • Same-store expenses grew 4.6% year-over-year due to higher controllable costs, offset by a 350 basis point decline in non-controllable expenses, which included real estate tax refunds.
  • The acquisition of The Lydian in Denver contributed to the company’s portfolio growth. The weighted-average debt cost stood at 3.6%, with a 5.6-year weighted-average maturity.

Q&A

  • Brad Heffern, RBC Capital, inquired about market trends in Minneapolis and Denver. Anne Olson acknowledged supply pressures but highlighted strong absorption, improved occupancy, and positive leasing trends in both markets.
  • John Kim, BMO, questioned blended leasing spread guidance. Campbell stated renewals are expected at 3%, with new leases in the high 1% to 2% range. Olson noted potential upside in renewal pricing due to high retention rates, depending on market dynamics.
  • Rob Stevenson, Janney, asked about expense drivers for 2025. Patel pointed to centralization benefits and a $900,000 reduction in insurance premiums.
  • Michael Gorman, BTIG, explored the Denver acquisition’s yield potential. Campbell outlined a path to mid-to-high 5% NOI yield through mark-to-market rent adjustments and operational efficiencies.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed mixed sentiments, focusing on market-specific risks and expense trends. Questions on Denver and Minneapolis revealed cautious optimism about leasing trends, while concerns about expense growth and transaction activity were notable.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks but adopted a measured stance during Q&A, particularly when discussing external growth and market challenges.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management displayed slightly more confidence in navigating market complexities but tempered expectations for leasing spreads.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Core FFO increased from $1.18 in Q3 to $1.21 in Q4, driven by NOI growth and improved occupancy. Dividend per share rose from $0.73 to $0.77.
  • Same-store revenue growth was consistent, with Q4 at 3.1% compared to Q3’s 3%. However, expense growth accelerated in Q4 due to controllable costs.
  • Blended leasing spreads declined to 0.45% in Q4 from 1.5% in Q3, reflecting seasonal and market-specific pressures.
  • Management’s tone shifted slightly from a focus on external acquisitions in Q3 to operational stability and cost efficiency in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Market-specific supply pressures in Denver and Minneapolis remain a challenge, with impacts on new lease pricing and occupancy.
  • Higher controllable expense growth, particularly in repairs and maintenance, could weigh on margins.
  • Interest rate volatility continues to limit transaction activity, affecting external growth opportunities.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the pace of rent growth and potential headwinds in expense management.

Final Takeaway

Centerspace demonstrated strong operational performance in Q4 2024, with growth in core FFO and occupancy. The company’s 2025 guidance reflects measured optimism, underpinned by stable leasing spreads and expense management initiatives. While market-specific supply pressures persist, the company’s focus on cost efficiencies and strategic portfolio adjustments positions it to navigate these challenges effectively.
